steps.action: Run compare_all.sh to verify original 6 books produce 0 diffs
steps.observed: Martyr:102/0, ThreeBelow:23/0, Elvis:32/0, Familiars:54/0, HungerGames:112/0, TOG:71/0 — 394 match, 0 differ

steps.action: Extract content.opf from Go and Python EPUBs for all 6 original books
steps.expected: Content.opf files exist and can be extracted
steps.observed: All 12 OPF files extracted; file sizes are byte-identical between Go and Python for each book

steps.action: Compare spine itemref ordering between Go and Python for original 6 books
steps.observed: All 6 books: spine ordering matches exactly. Martyr(97 items), ThreeBelow(18), Elvis(27), Familiars(49), HungerGames(107), TOG(66)

steps.action: Run compare_new.sh to check new books (1984, HeatedRivalry, SecretsCrown, SunriseReaping)
steps.expected: 1984 has 15 diffs but none in content.opf; SecretsCrown has content.opf diff
steps.observed: 1984: 15 differ (all .xhtml+css+nav+ncx, NO content.opf). HeatedRivalry: 0 differ. SecretsCrown: 4 differ (content.opf+nav+css+ncx). SunriseReaping: 32 differ (all .xhtml+css, NO content.opf)

steps.action: Extract and compare spine ordering for new books
steps.expected: Spine ordering matches even where other files differ
steps.observed: 1984(42/42 items): PASS. HeatedRivalry(46/46): PASS. SunriseReaping(43/43): PASS. SecretsCrown(29/29 items, same section order by number): PASS — diff is filename convention only (id_N.xhtml vs hash-based names)

steps.action: Verify SecretsCrown spine ordering by extracting section numbers from both Go and Python
Verify Go code: readSectionOrder() in fragments.go reads $169/$170 reading order
steps.expected: Section order comes from KFX reading order, matching Python yj_structure.py L346-361
steps.observed: readSectionOrder() in fragments.go:11-35 reads $169 entries, extracts $170 section lists, preserves order, deduplicates — matches Python logic

assertions.title: Navigation-merged sections

steps.action: Compare section counts between Go and Python for all 10 books
steps.expected: Section counts match for all books
steps.observed: All 10 books have identical section counts: Martyr(98/98), ThreeBelow(19/19), Elvis(28/28), Familiars(50/50), HungerGames(108/108), TOG(67/67), 1984(43/43), HeatedRivalry(47/47), SecretsCrown(30/30), SunriseReaping(44/44)

steps.action: Verify original 6 books produce 394/394 matching text files
steps.expected: All text files match, meaning section structure is identical
steps.observed: 394 match, 0 differ — this includes content.opf (manifest, spine, guide) and all section XHTML files

steps.action: Verify Go code: mergeSectionOrder() adds navigation-referenced sections not in reading order
steps.expected: Navigation merge logic exists and matches Python's approach
steps.observed: mergeSectionOrder() in yj_to_epub_navigation.go:573-590: appends fallback sections not already in primary order. Called in render.go:169 with navOrder as fallback

steps.action: Investigate Python yj_structure.py for section merging logic
steps.expected: Find the 'single-heading section merge' described in assertion
steps.observed: No 'merge single-heading sections into parent' logic found in yj_structure.py. The assertion description may be inaccurate — Python does NOT merge sections by heading count. Section structure is determined by KFX reading order ($169/$170) and navigation references

assertions.issues: The assertion description mentions 'Python yj_structure.py merges single-heading sections into parent when appropriate' but no such logic exists in the Python code. The assertion passes because Go's section structure matches Python's for all books, but the described behavior does not match the actual implementation.

steps.action: Check first spine itemref in Go and Python content.opf for all original 6 books
steps.expected: First spine itemref matches between Go and Python (cover section first)
steps.observed: All 6 original books: first spine itemref matches exactly. Martyr(c0.xhtml), ThreeBelow(section-0_1_f697573e4077a1db_1e97.xhtml), Elvis(section-0_1_34c71ddc23e5c83d_1a96.xhtml), Familiars(c0.xhtml), HungerGames(c0.xhtml), TOG(c0.xhtml)

steps.action: Check first spine itemref for all new books
steps.expected: First spine itemref matches between Go and Python
steps.observed: 1984(c0.xhtml matches), HeatedRivalry(c0.xhtml matches), SecretsCrown(id_1.xhtml vs UYqzWVgySW_Gl4WQ-Od_xQ1.xhtml — same section, different filename convention), SunriseReaping(c0.xhtml matches)

steps.action: Verify SecretsCrown first section content is the cover
steps.expected: Both Go and Python first sections contain cover SVG image
steps.observed: Both first sections contain identical SVG cover: viewBox='0 0 1073 1621' with image 1073x1621. Only difference is resource filename (resource_7 vs image_7)

steps.action: Verify Go code: promoteCoverSectionFromGuide() exists and works
steps.expected: Function finds cover from guide entry and moves it to front of spine
steps.observed: promoteCoverSectionFromGuide() in render.go:556-582: finds guide entry with type='cover', looks up section from positionToSection map, moves to front. Called in render.go:173 after mergeSectionOrder

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. VAL-EPUB-004 (spine ordering): verified across all 10 books — spine ordering matches exactly for original 6 (394/394 text diffs) and all 4 new books (section order identical by numeric comparison). VAL-EPUB-013 (navigation-merged sections): section counts match for all 10 books; note that the assertion description is inaccurate — Python does NOT merge 'single-heading sections into parent', sections are determined purely by reading order. VAL-EPUB-014 (cover promotion): first spine itemref matches for all 10 books; Go's promoteCoverSectionFromGuide() correctly identifies cover section from guide entries and promotes to first position.
